constraints: core.hooksPath=.husky — .git/hooks/ are ignored. All hook changes must go in .husky/ to be portable.
memory.json is always one commit behind in git but current on disk — post-commit writes it, next pre-commit stages it
Always run setup_github_environment BEFORE any git fetch/push/pull — token expires between sessions, never try unauthenticated first
When user asks a question, clarify intent BEFORE building — do not assume. Ask what they mean if ambiguous.
Before recommending a feature, grep/read the codebase to check if it already exists — read .nw-context inventory AND the actual files
Pre-commit hook blocks commits without .nw-session file and without at least 1 learning recorded — AI must follow CLAUDE.md startup checklist every session
[auto] ROOT-CAUSE FIRST: every fix commit MUST include root-cause in commit message. Protocol: (1) reproduce, (2) identify root cause, (3) fix, (4) verify coupled files.

SHIP COMMAND REQUIRED: Never manually git push + gh pr create + gh pr merge separately. Always use 'mycelium ship' which does auth→test→sync→squash→push→PR→merge atomically. The old workflow had two recurring failures: (1) auth token expires before push, (2) workflow stops after PR creation without merging.

VALUE GATE (mandatory): Before ANY multi-file change, answer in plain text: (1) What metric improves? (2) What bug is fixed? (3) What user-requested feature is added? If all three are NONE — STOP and tell the user 'this is cosmetic, should I skip it?' Never auto-proceed on refactors, reorganizations, or metaphor-driven restructuring.

NEVER claim something doesn't work without testing it first. When asked 'can X do Y?' — the answer is 'let me verify' not an assumption. Especially don't undersell to appear cautious — that's just as dishonest as overselling.

[auto] ROOT-CAUSE FIRST: every fix commit MUST include root-cause in commit message. Protocol: (1) reproduce, (2) identify root cause, (3) fix, (4) verify coupled files.

SHIP COMMAND REQUIRED: Never manually git push + gh pr create + gh pr merge separately. Always use 'mycelium ship' which does auth→test→sync→squash→push→PR→merge atomically. The old workflow had two recurring failures: (1) auth token expires before push, (2) workflow stops after PR creation without merging.

VALUE GATE (mandatory): Before ANY multi-file change, answer in plain text: (1) What metric improves? (2) What bug is fixed? (3) What user-requested feature is added? If all three are NONE — STOP and tell the user 'this is cosmetic, should I skip it?' Never auto-proceed on refactors, reorganizations, or metaphor-driven restructuring.

NEVER claim something doesn't work without testing it first. When asked 'can X do Y?' — the answer is 'let me verify' not an assumption. Especially don't undersell to appear cautious — that's just as dishonest as overselling.
